title: NA-Non ULIP
word_text:
https://www.bajajlifeinsurance.com/life-insurance-guide/tax/income-tax-challan-280-online-payment.html
How to Pay Income Tax ITNS 280 Online?
Paying income tax might seem intimidating, but you don't always need to hire an expensive chartered accountant to handle your taxes. By understanding the basics, you can easily manage the tax payment process on your own. One simple way to pay your taxes is by using Income Tax Challan 280.
What is ITNS 280?
Whether you prefer to pay your taxes online or offline, this form is required to complete the payment. Challan 280 is also referred to as ITNS 280.
You can use Challan 280 to pay various types of income taxes, including self-assessment tax, regular assessment tax, tax on distributed income and profit, advance tax, and surtax.
If you choose to pay ITNS 280 Challan offline, you need to visit a designated bank branch and make the payment there. However, if you decide to pay online, you can easily complete the transaction by visiting the TIN NSDL website, making the process quicker and more convenient[1]
Steps to Pay Income Tax Using ITNS 280 Online
Now that you understand the basics of Challan ITNS 280, let's go over the steps to make an Income Tax  Challan 280 online payment. Follow these simple instructions to complete the process:
Step 1: Visit the TIN NSDL Portal
Start by going to the official TIN NSDL website{ https://www.incometax.gov.in/iec/foportal/}.[2] [1]
Step 2: Select the ‘e-payment: Pay Taxes Online’ Option
On the homepage, navigate to the ‘Services’ tab and click on the ‘e-payment: Pay Taxes Online’ option.[1]
Step 3: Click the Provided Link
You will be directed to a new page where you will find a link. Click on it to proceed with the tax payment.[1]
Step 4: Redirected to the Income Tax e-Filing Portal
By clicking the link, you will be taken to the official Income Tax e-filing portal, where you can securely make your payment.[1]
Step 5: Select ‘e-Pay Tax’
Once on the portal, look for the ‘e-Pay Tax’ option on the left-hand side of the page. Click on it to continue with your payment.[1]
Step 6: Enter Your PAN or TAN
Next, you will be asked to provide either your PAN (Permanent Account Number) or TAN (Tax Deduction and Collection Account Number). After entering the details, click to confirm.[1]
Step 7: Verify with OTP
Enter your mobile number and wait for the OTP (One Time Password) sent to your phone. Input the OTP and click the ‘Continue’ button.[1]
Step 8: Choose ‘Income Tax’ as the Tax Type
On the next page, select the ‘Income Tax’ option and click ‘Proceed’ to move forward with the payment.[1]
Step 9: Fill in the Payment Details
You will now be asked to fill in the required details, such as the Type of Payment (e.g., Advance Tax, Self-Assessment Tax, or Tax on Regular Assessment), and the Assessment Year. Make sure the information is accurate.[1]
Step 10: Enter Tax Head and Amount
Next, under the Tax Head, input the amount you are required to pay and click ‘Continue’.[1]
Step 11: Select Payment Method
Choose your preferred payment mode—either Net Banking or Debit Card. Select the one that suits you.[1]
Step 12: Choose a Bank and Complete the Payment
After selecting your payment method, choose your bank from the drop-down list and complete the payment process.[1]
Step 13: Confirmation of Payment
Once the payment is processed, you will receive a Challan counterfoil that includes the Challan Identification Number (CIN), payment details, and the bank name. This serves as confirmation that your payment was successful.[1]
Verification of Challan Tax Payment
The Income Tax Department (ITD) allows taxpayers to verify the status of their Challan tax payments. You can easily check the payment status by logging into the NSDL website.
CIN-based view:
Enter the Challan Identification Number (CIN), challan tender date, BSR code of the bank branch, and the serial number of the challan to track its status. You can also confirm the tax amount by providing the actual paid amount.[2]
TAN-based view:
Provide your Tax Deduction and Collection Number (TAN) and challan tender date to view your CIN details. You can also validate the tax amount uploaded by the bank for a specific CIN.[2]
Benefits of Paying Income Tax Online Using ITNS 280
Convenience & Time-saving:
Online payment allows taxpayers to pay from anywhere, anytime, with just an internet connection. It eliminates the need to visit a tax office or deal with manual paperwork, making the process quick and hassle-free[2]
Accuracy & Instant Confirmation:
Online platforms often include built-in income tax calculators to ensure accurate tax payments. Taxpayers also receive instant confirmation of successful payments, providing peace of mind.[2]
Security & Verification:
Reputable online payment systems use advanced security measures to protect financial information. You can also verify payment status through the Tax Information Network (TIN) website for confirmation.[2]
Conclusion
Paying your income tax online using ITNS 280 is an efficient, secure, and hassle-free process. By following the simple steps outlined in this guide, you can easily make your tax payments and stay compliant with the Income Tax Act. Make sure to check your payment details carefully, keep the receipt for reference, and complete your payments on time to avoid penalties. Whether you're paying advance tax, self-assessment tax, or any other tax, the online payment system makes the entire process easier than ever before.
Frequently Asked Questions
What is ITNS 280?
ITNS 280 is a challan form used for paying income tax, including self-assessment tax, advance tax, and TDS (Tax Deducted at Source).
How can I pay income tax using ITNS 280 online?
To pay income tax online using ITNS 280, you must visit the Income Tax e-Filing portal, fill out the challan form, select your payment method, and complete the payment.
Which taxes can be paid using ITNS 280?
ITNS 280 can be used to pay various types of taxes, including income tax, advance tax, self-assessment tax, and TDS.
Can I pay income tax through net banking?
Yes, net banking is one of the payment methods available when paying income tax through ITNS 280 online.
What is the Challan Identification Number (CIN)?
The CIN is a unique number generated once you complete the payment. It serves as proof of your tax payment.
How do I know if my payment is successful?
Once your payment is processed, you will receive an acknowledgment receipt containing the CIN, which confirms that your payment was successful.
Can I pay taxes for a previous year using ITNS 280?
Yes, you can select the relevant assessment year when filling out the ITNS 280 form.
Disclaimers:
The content provided is for general informational purposes only. The material is compiled from publicly available sources, internal insights, and other information deemed reliable.
